texto: Life is plenty pointless. We're all put here on this earth, such a minuscule thing, really. This tiny Earth, in the midst of an ever-larger universe. We were put here for reasons unbeknownst to us, reasons that may not even exist. And, above all that, we try so hard. People work to discover things, to try to create the greatest new thing, to learn everything, to find answers. But those answers are pointless. Because one day, this world will end. And whatever mark you may have thought you left will vanish, like it was never even there. And that, my friends, is the meaning of life.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Payton Webb: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Life is plenty pointless. We're all put here on this earth, such a minuscule thing, really. This …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, death, earth pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.
